message:
🌱 Add consul: Consul Connect - Ability to configure envoy access_log_path mission (#1201)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "consul-5231-consul-connect-ability-to-configure-envoy-access-log-path",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "consul: Consul Connect - Ability to configure envoy access_log_path",
+    "description": "Consul Connect - Ability to configure envoy access_log_path. Requested by 32+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current consul deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your consul version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
helm list -n consul 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working consul installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review consul configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant consul configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get configmap -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=consul
```
#### Feature Description

The access_log_path for Envoy currently defaults to /dev/null. It'd be great if this was configurable with Consul Connect.

#### Use Case(s)

Output envoy access logs to stdout or saving them to a local file."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Consul Connect - Ability to configure envoy access_log_path",
+        "description": "Reopening to capture that we want to be able to configure the access log path for both the TCPProxy filters and the HTTP Connect Manager within the xDS package in addition to the access logs for the admin interface.
```yaml
static_resources:
  listeners:
  - name: inbound_10000
    address:
      socket_address:
        protocol: TCP
        address: 0.0.0.0
        port_value: 10000
    traffic_direction: INBOUND
    filter_chains:
    - filters:
      - name: envoy.filters.network.http_connection_manager
        typed_config:
          "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.config.filter.network.http_connection_manager.v2.HttpConnectionManager
          access_log:
          - name: envoy.access_loggers.file
            config:
              path: /dev/stdout
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get events -n consul --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Consul Connect - Ability to configure envoy access_log_path" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Reopening to capture that we want to be able to configure the access log path for both the TCPProxy filters and the HTTP Connect Manager within the xDS package in addition to the access logs for the admin interface.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "static_resources:
  listeners:
  - name: inbound_10000
    address:
      socket_address:
        protocol: TCP
        address: 0.0.0.0
        port_value: 10000
    traffic_direction: INBOUND
    filter_chains:
    - filters:
      - name: envoy.filters.network.http_connection_manager
        typed_config:
          "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.config.filter.network.http_connection_manager.v2.HttpConnectionManager
          access_log:
          - name: envoy.access_loggers.file
            config:
              path: /dev/stdout",
+        "envoy_public_listener_json= <<EOL
             {
                "name": "local_agent",
                "address": {
                  "socket_address": {
                    "address": "0.0.0.0",
					"port_value": 20002
                  }
                },
                "filter_chains": [
                 {
                   "filters": [
                   {
                     "name": "envoy.tcp_proxy",
                     "config": {
					   "cluster": "redis01",
                       "stat_prefix": "envoy.tcp_proxy",
                     "tracing": {
                       "random_sampling": {}
                     },                     
                    "access_log": {
                      "name": "envoy.file_access_log",
                      "typed_config": {
         "
+      ]
+    }
+  },
